resources.string: No news yet. Check back soon!
Your Minecraft companion for crafting, building, and exploring
What do you have planned for today?
Tap to open your Todo list \u2192
Full database with categories, durability, and recipes
Calculate the cheapest XP order for combining books on the anvil
Tap any item, mob, biome, or structure to jump to its detail page
Global search across blocks, mobs, items, recipes, and more
Stream Minecraft world data from your PC
Both devices on the same WiFi
Clear all cached Connect data? Your device pairing will be kept.
Track your progress through all Minecraft advancements. Syncs live when connected to Spyglass Connect.
Spyglass Connect can collect anonymous data to help improve the app. No personal information is ever shared.
Automatic error reporting to fix bugs
Show ads based on your interests. Non-personalized ads still appear if disabled.
Which tab opens first when you tap Browse
Which tab opens first when you tap Tools
Daily Minecraft tip on the Home screen
Display your favorited items on the Home page
Show mini clock in the top bar
No favorites yet. Star items in the Browse tabs to add them here.
This will permanently delete all your todos, notes, waypoints, shopping lists, favorites, and advancement progress. This cannot be undone.
Filter content by Minecraft edition and version
Stream Minecraft world data from your PC over local WiFi. Pairing is managed from the Connect screen.
Check for updated game data and textures
Stream Minecraft world data from your PC over local WiFi
Paired device info and connection settings are managed from the Connect screen.
Download Spyglass Connect for your computer:
Clear all cached Connect data for every world? Your device pairing will be kept.
Used to auto-select your player in Connect
Hide blocks that can\'t be obtained in Survival mode
Include features from Minecraft experimental data packs (e.g. Librarian trade rebalance)
Adjust text size throughout the app
Which screen opens when you launch the app
Choose the display language for Spyglass. Game data translations are downloaded separately.
Show translated names and descriptions for blocks, items, mobs, and other game data
Display the English name alongside translations for reference
Require biometric or PIN to open the app
How often to check for updated game data
Free up space by removing cached textures
Leave a review to support Spyglass Connect
See what changed in recent updates
Buy Me a Cup of Coffee
Not affiliated with Mojang Studios or Microsoft.

All game data is stored locally on your device. Spyglass Connect displays ads via Google AdMob and may collect anonymous analytics and crash reports with your consent. No personal information is ever shared.

Spyglass Connect is an unofficial, fan-made companion application for Minecraft: Java Edition.

This app is NOT an official Minecraft product. It is NOT approved by or associated with Mojang Studios, Microsoft Corporation, or any of their subsidiaries or affiliates.

"Minecraft" is a trademark of Mojang Studios. All Minecraft-related content, including but not limited to game data, item names, mob names, biome names, and block names, is the intellectual property of Mojang Studios.

All game data displayed in this application is sourced from publicly available information on the Minecraft Wiki and official Mojang resources. This app does not modify, interact with, or connect to Minecraft game servers in any way.

Pixel art textures used in this app are from the "Pixel Perfection" resource pack by XSSheep, licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0. Entity icons are from the "Entity-Icons" project, licensed under CC0 1.0.

This application is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. The developer assumes no responsibility for any issues arising from the use of this application.
You are granted a non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to use the Spyglass Connect application as distributed through official release channels for personal, non-commercial purposes.
You may not copy, reproduce, or duplicate the source code or application.
You may not modify, adapt, translate, or create derivative works.
You may not distribute, sublicense, lease, rent, or transfer the application to any third party.
You may not reverse-engineer, decompile, or disassemble the application.
You may not use the source code or application for any commercial purpose.
This application includes third-party assets subject to their own licenses, as noted on the About page.
The software is provided \"as is\", without warranty of any kind. The developer assumes no responsibility for any issues arising from the use of this application.
Your feedback helps make Spyglass Connect better for everyone. Thank you!
Type at least 2 characters to search blocks, mobs, biomes, structures, items, recipes, trades, and more.
Every placeable block with hardness and tool info
Non-block items: tools, weapons, armor, food, materials, and more -- with sources and cross-links
Try a different search or category
Crafting recipes for every craftable item
Every Minecraft mob with health, drops, spawn biomes, and strategy tips
Try a different search or category
Every Minecraft biome with temperature, mobs, structures, and map colors
Every Minecraft structure with biomes, mobs, loot, and how to find them
Try a different search or dimension filter
All enchantments by target, rarity, and compatibility
Try a different search or filter
Brewing paths and effects for every potion
Villager trades by profession and level
Try a different search or profession
Track your progress through all Minecraft advancements with practical guides
Try a different search or filter
Clear all advancement checkmarks? This cannot be undone.
All Minecraft Java 1.21.4 commands with syntax, descriptions, and permission levels.
Try a different search or filter
1. Open Spyglass Connect on your computer\n2. Make sure both devices are on the same WiFi\n3. Tap \'Scan QR Code\' above and point at the QR on screen
Viewing other players\' data may not align with fair play on multiplayer worlds.
Tap your player to set them as default for this world
Point at the QR code on your computer
Not a Spyglass Connect QR code
Camera permission is required to scan QR codes
Showing 15 of %d results \u2014 refine your search
Connect to a world to auto-detect locations
Locations will appear when world data loads
No shopping lists yet \u2014 create one in the Shopping tab

Enter how many items you need to smelt and see exactly how much of each fuel type you\'ll need. Green dots mean high efficiency, gold is mid, gray is low. Type amounts like \"14 stacks\", \"5k\", or \"2 chests\".
Cheat sheets for ores, XP, brewing, redstone, times, and dye colors
Calculate comparator signal strength from container fill levels. Plan exact item counts for redstone contraptions.
Common: 64 (most items), 16 (eggs, ender pearls, snowballs), 1 (tools, armor)
Signal 15 if has eye, 0 if empty
A Redstone Comparator reads the fill level of a container behind it and outputs a signal strength from 0-15.
Output = rear signal if rear >= side signal, otherwise 0.
Output = rear signal - side signal (minimum 0). Useful for arithmetic circuits.
signal = floor(1 + (sum of item fractions / total slots) * 14)\nwhere item fraction = count / max_stack_size for each slot\nSignal is 0 when empty, 15 when completely full.
Write, label, and organize your Minecraft notes. Add labels to group them by topic.
Try a different search or label
Tap + to create your first note

Generate mazes for Minecraft. Choose rectangular for classic hedge mazes, circular for concentric ring mazes, or multi-floor for stacked mazes connected by staircases. Use the layer slider to build layer by layer, or switch to 3D for an isometric preview.

Enter the width, length, and height of an area to calculate how many blocks you need to fill it. Toggle between blocks and chunks for large builds. Results show storage needed in stacks, chests, and shulker boxes.

Pick an item and select the enchantments you want. This tool calculates the cheapest XP order to combine them on an anvil, avoiding the \"Too Expensive\" cap. Tap enchantment levels (I\u2013V) to adjust.

Every time an item is used in an anvil, it gains a hidden \"anvil uses\" counter. The penalty doubles each time: 0, 1, 3, 7, 15, 31 levels (formula: 2\u207F \u2212 1). This applies to both the target and sacrifice items.
If any single anvil operation costs 40+ levels, the game blocks it entirely. After 6 anvil uses, the penalty alone hits 63 levels \u2014 making further enchanting impossible.

The output item\u2019s anvil uses = max(target, sacrifice) + 1. By combining books in pairs first (binary tree), each book accumulates fewer anvil uses. Cheapest enchantments go first so their small base costs absorb the growing penalty. This calculator finds that optimal order automatically.
Save and organize coordinates for your important Minecraft locations.
Try a different search or filter
Tap + to save your first location
Track Minecraft\u2019s day/night cycle and upcoming events.
Not synced \u2014 choose a method below
Enter the tick value from the F3 debug screen
Tap when you see the event in-game:
No events configured. Tap below to add events.
1. Press F3 in Minecraft to open the debug screen
2. Find the \u201cDay Time\u201d value on the left side
3. Enter the tick number (0\u201323999) in the field above
4. Tap \u201cSync\u201d \u2014 the clock will track from that point
Tap the Sunrise, Noon, or Sunset button the moment you see that event happen in-game.
\u2022 Sunrise \u2014 when the sun first appears on the horizon
\u2022 Noon \u2014 when the sun is directly overhead
\u2022 Sunset \u2014 when the sun starts to dip below the horizon
The event timeline shows how long until each event occurs.
Events are color-coded: green (safe), gold (warning), red (danger).
All predefined events are already added.
Enter the current in-game day from the F3 debug screen (Day line). You can update this any time after a server restart or crash.
Complete food reference with hunger, saturation, and special effects. Sort by saturation to find the best foods.
Saturation is a hidden stat that determines how long before hunger starts decreasing. Higher saturation = longer before you get hungry again. Your saturation cannot exceed your current hunger level.
Track tasks, gather goals, and link to your tools
All 18 armor trim templates, 11 trim materials, and where to find them.
Each material changes the color of the trim pattern on the armor.
If the trim material matches the armor material (e.g., Diamond trim on Diamond armor), the pattern uses a darker color palette.

1. Open the Smithing Table
2. Place a Trim Template in the left slot
3. Place the armor piece in the middle slot
4. Place the trim material in the right slot
5. Take the trimmed armor from the output

• Templates are consumed during trimming
• Trims are purely cosmetic (no gameplay effect)
• You can re-trim armor (overwrites the previous pattern)
• Enchantments are preserved when trimming
• Works on all armor materials

In a crafting table:
1 Template + 7 Diamonds + 1 Duplication Material = 2 Templates

Each template has a specific duplication material (see the Templates tab). This makes rare templates renewable after finding the first one.
Design custom banners like a Loom. Pick a base color, add up to 6 pattern layers, and preview your design live.
These items unlock special patterns in the Loom. They are not consumed when used.
These patterns are available in the Loom without any special banner pattern item.
Any of these colors can be used in the Loom to apply patterns to banners.

1. Place a banner in the top-left slot
2. Place a dye in the top-right slot
3. Optionally place a pattern item in the middle slot
4. Select a pattern from the list
5. Take the result from the output slot

• Banners can have up to 6 pattern layers
• Pattern items are NOT consumed when used
• You can copy a banner by placing it + blank banner in a crafting grid
• Banners can be placed on shields at a crafting table
Build perfect shapes in Minecraft. Set a radius and height, then use the Y-layer slider to see exactly which blocks to place on each layer, or switch to 3D for an isometric preview.
Calculate torch and light source spacing to prevent hostile mob spawning.
Place %1$s every %2$d blocks along a line (hallway, tunnel).
Place %1$s in a grid pattern every %2$d blocks in both X and Z directions to fully prevent mob spawning on a flat surface.
%1$s is too dim (level %2$d) for effective mob-proofing. Light must reach level 1+ at every block. Consider a brighter source.
Hostile mobs spawn at block light level 0 only. Any light level 1 or above prevents spawning.
Most hostile mobs spawn at light level 11 or lower. Blazes and Wither Skeletons spawn in fortresses at light level 11 or lower.
Spawner blocks activate when light level is 11 or lower. Place light sources within 4 blocks to disable them.
Light decreases by 1 for each block of distance (Manhattan distance). A torch at level 14 reaches level 1 at 13 blocks away, and level 0 at 14 blocks.
Plan your gathering and crafting with named lists
Create a list to start tracking items
This will permanently remove this list and all its items.
Save coordinates above to track your portals
Convert coordinates between the Overworld and Nether (8:1 ratio), calculate how much obsidian you need for a custom-sized portal, or save portal locations to keep track of your network.
Librarian enchantments are biome-locked when the Villager Trade Rebalance data pack is enabled. Find the right biome for the enchantment you need.
Requires the Villager Trade Rebalance data pack. Not active in default gameplay.
Guaranteed enchanted book trade at Master level.
Available at Novice through Expert levels.
A librarian\'s available enchantments are determined by the biome they first claimed a lectern in. Moving them to a different biome does NOT change their enchantment pool.

Some enchantments are capped below their maximum level from librarians:
• Protection III (max IV requires anvil combining)
• Fortune II (max III requires anvil combining)
• Unbreaking II (max III requires anvil combining)

• For Mending, breed villagers in a Swamp biome
• For Silk Touch, use a Snowy biome
• Combine capped enchantments via anvil for max level
• Break and replace the lectern to reroll trades

• Trident: Channeling, Loyalty, Riptide, Impaling
• Crossbow: Multishot, Piercing, Quick Charge
• Fishing Rod: Luck of the Sea, Lure
• Treasure Only: Soul Speed, Swift Sneak, Wind Burst
• 1.21+ Only: Breach, Density (Mace enchantments)
These must be found from loot chests, fishing, mob drops, or enchanting tables.

Figure out how many chests and shulker boxes you need for a given quantity of items. Pick an item from the dropdown to also see block compression (e.g. how many iron blocks you can make from your iron ingots).
Notable and exclusive loot items found in each Minecraft structure. Find the right structure for what you need.
Shows when connected to a PC via Spyglass Connect
%1$d blocks in this layer • X %2$d–%3$d Z %4$d–%5$d
